title: Treasury Securities - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This page describes the New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk operations, including management of the System Open Market Account (SOMA) portfolio, Treasury outright purchases/sales, and rollover operations executed with primary dealers as directed by the FOMC. The Fed also provides limited Treasury transaction services to approximately 200 central banks and official institutions.
key_points: The New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk executes Treasury securities transactions for the SOMA portfolio as directed by the FOMC
Transactions are executed exclusively with primary dealers in the secondary market
SOMA holdings have been expanded on multiple occasions since end of 2008 to implement monetary policy
Other securities in the SOMA portfolio include agency MBS, agency debt, and agency CMBS
Transaction data is published quarterly, approximately two years after execution, per Dodd-Frank Act requirements
The New York Fed provides Treasury transaction services to approximately 200 central banks and official institutions
why_it_matters: Understanding the Fed's Treasury market operations is foundational to understanding Treasury market structure and liquidity provision. The Desk's operations represent a significant flow in the Treasury market, and primary dealer relationships form a core part of the market's institutional architecture.
market_structure_context: This source describes core market structure elements: the role of primary dealers as counterparties to Fed operations, the SOMA portfolio as a mechanism for Fed Treasury holdings, and the institutional framework through which the Federal Reserve participates in Treasury markets. The relationship between the FOMC, the Desk, and primary dealers represents key market infrastructure.
